title: Implement MeshCore stream framing and adapter dispatcher
description: Why this exists: individual MeshCore operations need a common bounded framing, decode, dispatch, and response path. What needs to be done: implement reassembly, frame validation, output queueing, dispatch table, request correlation if required, and explicit unknown/unsupported handling. Acceptance criteria: tests cover valid frames, split frames, malformed length/checksum, oversized frames, unknown commands, disconnect cleanup, and output backpressure; dispatcher routes supported MVP operations to stub handlers; MeshCore RF packets are not passed to LICHEN LoRa as MeshCore RF.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.3 codec strategy: split MeshCore serial/TCP framing from the inner byte-command codec. BLE NUS passes one raw inner frame per GATT value; serial/TCP uses 0x3c/0x3e + uint16_le length with bounded resync. Dispatcher should consume typed codec views and preserve unknown valid command bytes for deterministic ERR_UNSUPPORTED.
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.13 command matrix: dispatcher phase should define constants for all commands 0x01..0x41, preserve valid-but-unsupported command IDs, and return deterministic ERR_UNSUPPORTED for every unsupported command. Initial supported reads should include APP_START, DEVICE_QUERY, GET_CONTACTS empty, GET_CHANNEL placeholder, SYNC_NEXT_MESSAGE empty, GET_BATT_AND_STORAGE placeholder, GET_DEVICE_TIME, GET_CUSTOM_VARS empty, GET_AUTOADD_CONFIG disabled, and GET_DEFAULT_FLOOD_SCOPE null.
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.4 implementation: MeshCore BLE transport now exposes ble_meshcore_dequeue_rx(), ble_meshcore_enqueue_tx_if_session(), session epoch guards, and reset helpers. Dispatcher implementation must provide the production consumer/pump for these queues; project-LICHEN-9c4y.4 deliberately stops at the transport boundary and does not process commands or generate responses.
